description:
You are a cloud-architecture consultant who has just completed a lift-and-shift migration of several internal micro-services.  
Quality-assurance now needs an **analytics bundle** that summarises what happened during each service’s migration run and extracts every single failure for a post-mortem review.  All raw logs are already on disk; you only have to crunch them.

STARTING SITUATION
──────────────────
1. Directory tree
   /home/user/cloud_migration/
   ├── logs/              ← contains nothing except the four *.log* files listed below  
   └── (no other sub-directories at the moment)

2. Log files and their exact contents  
   a) /home/user/cloud_migration/logs/auth-service_20231015.log  
      [2023-10-15 10:00:01] auth-service mig-001 SUCCESS 450  
      [2023-10-15 10:05:22] auth-service mig-002 FAILED 300  
      [2023-10-15 10:15:10] auth-service mig-003 SUCCESS 500  

   b) /home/user/cloud_migration/logs/payment-service_20231015.log  
      [2023-10-15 10:01:30] payment-service mig-010 SUCCESS 800  
      [2023-10-15 10:07:45] payment-service mig-011 SUCCESS 750  
      [2023-10-15 10:11:05] payment-service mig-012 FAILED 900  
      [2023-10-15 10:12:55] payment-service mig-013 FAILED 880  

   c) /home/user/cloud_migration/logs/auth-service_20231016.log  
      [2023-10-16 11:00:11] auth-service mig-004 SUCCESS 460  
      [2023-10-16 11:05:55] auth-service mig-005 FAILED 350  

   d) /home/user/cloud_migration/logs/payment-service_20231016.log  
      [2023-10-16 11:02:01] payment-service mig-014 SUCCESS 780  

3. Line structure (every single line in every file follows this pattern):  
   [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] <service_name> <migration_id> <status> <elapsed_ms>  
   • <status> is either SUCCESS or FAILED  
   • <elapsed_ms> is an integer (milliseconds)

YOUR TASK
─────────
Produce an **analytics bundle** inside a new directory called  
   /home/user/cloud_migration/reports

The bundle must contain the three artefacts below, all created exclusively from the *.log* files listed above:

1. /home/user/cloud_migration/reports/migration_summary.csv  
   • Must be exactly five comma-separated fields with this header line (case-sensitive):  
     service_name,total_migrations,successful_migrations,failed_migrations,average_elapsed_ms  
   • One subsequent line per distinct <service_name>, sorted alphabetically by service name.  
   • average_elapsed_ms is the arithmetic mean of all elapsed_ms values for that service, rounded to the nearest *integer* using standard rounding rules.  
   • No extra spaces, no trailing commas, and must end with a final newline (Unix style).

2. /home/user/cloud_migration/reports/failed_migrations.log  
   • Contains only those raw log lines where <status> == FAILED.  
   • Lines must be sorted in **strict chronological order** based on their timestamps (earliest first).  
   • Each line must be copied verbatim from the originating file, including the square brackets, spaces and line endings.  
   • File must end with a single trailing newline (Unix style).

3. /home/user/cloud_migration/reports/analysis.log  
   • A free-form command log: append one line **every time** you run a shell command while solving the task.  
   • Each entry must follow the exact format  
        [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] CMD: <the_command_you_ran>  
     where the timestamp is captured *immediately before* you press ENTER.  
   • Do **not** record blank lines or comments, only real commands.  
   • analysis.log must have at least three lines by the time you finish.

CONSTRAINTS & HINTS
───────────────────
• You cannot install new software beyond what a standard Debian/Ubuntu container provides (awk, grep, sed, sort, cut, paste, xargs, parallel, etc. are available).  
• You do NOT have root, so stay completely inside /home/user.  
• The task can be achieved with a combination of GNU coreutils, awk (or sed), and whichever parallelisation tool you like. Feel free to background jobs or use GNU parallel, but remember to capture everything you actually type in analysis.log.  
• Do **not** modify or delete the original log files.  
• Ensure the reports/ directory exists before you start writing files inside it; automated tests will fail if the directory is missing or wrongly named.  
• Be careful with quoting: the CSV must have exactly four commas per data line, no quotes.  
• Before finishing, run `cat /home/user/cloud_migration/reports/migration_summary.csv` and  
  `cat /home/user/cloud_migration/reports/failed_migrations.log` to visually verify correctness; seeing the console output is fine.  
• When you are satisfied, simply exit—automated graders will verify the three artefacts byte-for-byte.

EXPECTED EXAMPLE (for illustration only; grader will use exact byte comparison)
─────────────────
migration_summary.csv:
service_name,total_migrations,successful_migrations,failed_migrations,average_elapsed_ms
auth-service,5,3,2,412
payment-service,5,3,2,822

failed_migrations.log:
[2023-10-15 10:05:22] auth-service mig-002 FAILED 300
[2023-10-15 10:11:05] payment-service mig-012 FAILED 900
[2023-10-15 10:12:55] payment-service mig-013 FAILED 880
[2023-10-16 11:05:55] auth-service mig-005 FAILED 350

analysis.log:
[2024-03-17 09:41:22] CMD: mkdir -p /home/user/cloud_migration/reports
[2024-03-17 09:42:01] CMD: awk ...
[...]

Only the existence, ordering and exact formatting of the three files will be automatically assessed.
truth:
Pre-existing artefacts
----------------------
1. The file system already contains the following directory and files with **exactly** the indicated byte contents (newline = 0x0A):

/home/user/cloud_migration/logs/auth-service_20231015.log  
[2023-10-15 10:00:01] auth-service mig-001 SUCCESS 450\n
[2023-10-15 10:05:22] auth-service mig-002 FAILED 300\n
[2023-10-15 10:15:10] auth-service mig-003 SUCCESS 500\n

/home/user/cloud_migration/logs/payment-service_20231015.log  
[2023-10-15 10:01:30] payment-service mig-010 SUCCESS 800\n
[2023-10-15 10:07:45] payment-service mig-011 SUCCESS 750\n
[2023-10-15 10:11:05] payment-service mig-012 FAILED 900\n
[2023-10-15 10:12:55] payment-service mig-013 FAILED 880\n

/home/user/cloud_migration/logs/auth-service_20231016.log  
[2023-10-16 11:00:11] auth-service mig-004 SUCCESS 460\n
[2023-10-16 11:05:55] auth-service mig-005 FAILED 350\n

/home/user/cloud_migration/logs/payment-service_20231016.log  
[2023-10-16 11:02:01] payment-service mig-014 SUCCESS 780\n

Expected artefacts (grader will check for **exact** byte-for-byte match)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/cloud_migration/reports/migration_summary.csv  
   Contents (including final trailing newline):  
service_name,total_migrations,successful_migrations,failed_migrations,average_elapsed_ms\n
auth-service,5,3,2,412\n
payment-service,5,3,2,822\n

2. /home/user/cloud_migration/reports/failed_migrations.log  
   Contents (including final trailing newline):  
[2023-10-15 10:05:22] auth-service mig-002 FAILED 300\n
[2023-10-15 10:11:05] payment-service mig-012 FAILED 900\n
[2023-10-15 10:12:55] payment-service mig-013 FAILED 880\n
[2023-10-16 11:05:55] auth-service mig-005 FAILED 350\n

3. /home/user/cloud_migration/reports/analysis.log  
   • File must exist and be readable.  
   • Must contain at least 3 lines, each matching the regexp  
     ^\\[[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\\] CMD: .+  
   • Grader will not enforce the specific commands, only the format and minimum count.

Permissions
-----------
All existing files are world-readable (mode 0644).  
The agent will have write permission to /home/user/cloud_migration/ and anything it creates there.
